item.title: Big Banks Shake Off CRE Jitters, Cut Loss Reserves And Raise AI Hopes
item.description: The clouds of debt maturities and nonperforming loans haven’t entirely cleared from the country’s largest banks, but the shine of artificial intelligence and data center development is erasing the shadows cast by Wall Street's anxieties over pandemic-era...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: matthew.wasielewski@bisnow.com

item.title: Largest U.S. Grid Operator Sees Power Costs Soar By 60% Due To Data Center Development
item.description: The price of power is set to jump more than 60% for PJM, the largest electrical grid in the country, amid insatiable demand from data centers. “Demand for electricity continues to grow faster than electricity supply,” PJM CEO David Mills said in...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: matthew.wasielewski@bisnow.com

item.title: DHS Says 470K SF Warehouse It Planned To Sell Will Become Immigrant Jail After All
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security has reversed course on its plan to sell a New Jersey warehouse and will go back to its original intention of converting it into a detention facility. A federal judge was notified July 10 of the agency’s decision...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: kayla.carmicheal@bisnow.com

item.title: FIRST DRAFT LIVE: 'Not Happy, But Comfortable': CREFC President On CRE Finding A New Normal
item.description: Bisnow’s First Draft Live is a regular series featuring live conversations about the critical stories impacting CRE right now. It is a companion to The First Draft, Bisnow’s daily, flagship CRE newsletter. Register here to get The First Draft in your...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: julia.troy@bisnow.com

item.title: Palm Beach County Pauses Data Center Applications
item.description: South Florida has entered the data center debate. In one of the first moves to regulate the construction of the asset class in the region, the Palm Beach County Commission paused data center proposals this week until it adopts appropriate zoning, following pushback from residents. Commissioners this week voted 5-2 to freeze new data center […]
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Julia Echikson

item.title: More States Implement Special Power Rates For Data Centers
item.description: As the data center building boom fuels record electricity demand, state leaders are aiming to prevent the industry’s skyrocketing power consumption from creating higher power bills for households and businesses. In recent weeks, a series of states...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: dan.rabb@bisnow.com

item.title: Aston Power Secures $20M Series A Funding
item.description: As exponential AI growth drives data center expansion nationally, proptech has moved into the sector to help provide the vast amount of power needed to operate those facilities straining to keep up with demand. item.title: Suburban Philly Office Gets $17.8M Refi After Distribution Tenant Expands Footprint

item.author: Google News Data Centers NJ/PA/FL

item.title: If You Cannot Explain the Water, You Cannot Build the Data Center
item.description: Water has become the defining issue in the data center debate. Not because it is misunderstood, but because it is experienced locally, immediately and, in many cases, under stress. Communities do not need to be convinced that data centers use water. They want to know how much, from where, and at what cost to everything […]
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Tom Acitelli

item.description: Deugen flips Cranbury IOS site after securing key entitlements for 16-acre property RE-NJ

item.title: Ezeflow USA-Flowline Expands New Castle, Pennsylvania, Manufacturing Operations
item.description: Metal fittings manufacturer Ezeflow USA-Flowline plans to expand its operations in New Castle, Pennsylvania. The $6.5 million project is expected to create 30 jobs over the next four years.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Area Development Master
